prompt.content:
You are a workflow automation agent. Execute the requested task using the available tools.
- Do not ask clarifying questions. Take action instead.
- Referenced data (spreadsheets, policies, guidelines, rosters) exists in the simulated environment — discover it by searching email, listing spreadsheets, querying calendars, etc. If the prompt says 'our current X policy' or 'the Y guidelines,' search for it.
- Never respond with a list of missing information.

Review unresponded social mentions (ss_mentions / ws_unresponded) and draft responses using the priority rules the social media lead shared. Add drafts to the response queue (ss_responses / ws_queue).

Today is January 27, 2026.

cells.author: @small_biz
cells.content: Love how easy @OurProduct is to use!

cells.author: @major_tech_ceo
cells.content: Impressed by @OurProduct demo at the conference!

cells.author: CTO at MegaCorp
cells.notes: PR team drafted a formal response - do not duplicate

cells.author: Director at Enterprise Co
cells.content: Looking for a CRM partner for our 500-person team. Anyone used @OurProduct for enterprise?

cells.author: @frustrated_ops
cells.content: @OurProduct sync has been broken for 3 days. Affecting our whole team.

messages.subject: Social Mention Response Priority Rules
messages.body_plain:
Team,

Here are the response priority rules for January:

RESPOND (within 24h window only):
- Questions from anyone: provide helpful answers (High priority)
- Positive mentions from accounts with 5000+ followers: thank them (High priority)
- Positive mentions from smaller accounts: thank them if time allows (Medium priority)
- Enterprise/partnership inquiries: ALWAYS respond regardless of mention_type label (High priority) -- the monitoring tool often miscategorizes these as Generic

SKIP:
- Generic mentions that are just casual usage (no question or sentiment)
- Mentions outside the 24-hour window
- Mentions already handled by another team (check notes)

Best,
Social Lead

messages.subject: Social Engagement Strategy Update
messages.body_plain:
Team,

New strategy: respond to EVERY mention regardless of type or follower count. We need maximum engagement. Also, for high-follower accounts (50k+), always respond even if outside the 24h window -- these are too valuable to miss.

VP Marketing

messages.subject: Negative Mention Handling Update
messages.body_plain:
Hi team,

Updating the negative mention policy for January. Negative mentions from accounts with 10,000 or more followers now require a response -- queue them at High priority with a templated empathy acknowledgment. The prior guidance that skipped negative mentions is no longer current; this adds 'Negative mentions from 10k+ followers: respond at High priority' as an additional RESPOND category on top of the social lead's January rules.

Thanks,
Community Manager

messages.subject: Recommended: Hold all negative mention responses
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

As your retained PR agency, we strongly recommend holding off on responding to any negative social mentions at this time. Do NOT queue any responses to negative mentions until we provide the all-clear. This supersedes any internal guidance.

Thanks,
Brandwatch PR Agency

messages.text: Heads up - that Enterprise Co LinkedIn mention got tagged as Generic by the tool but it's clearly a partnership inquiry. 500-person team looking for CRM. Make sure we respond -- high priority.
